summary: Epic second‑set marathon – He's Gone > Truckin' > The Other One > Eyes > China Doll
blurb: The Feb 19 1973 second set is a nonstop jam marathon, highlighted by the He’s Gone → Truckin’ → The Other One → Eyes Of The World → China Doll sequence. Jerry’s tone and Phil’s bass are in top form, and the band rides the groove through Sugar Magnolia and Casey Jones to close.
review: On February 19, 1973 at Chicago’s International Amphitheatre the Grateful Dead delivered a legendary second‑set that still feels fresh. The show kicks off with a scorching “He’s Gone” that launches straight into an incandescent “Truckin’”, then rolls through a wild “The Other One”. From there the band builds a soaring 20‑minute medley – “Eyes Of The World” morphing seamlessly into “China Doll” – showcasing Jerry Garcia’s flawless tone and vocal delivery, Phil Lesh’s “kicking ass” bass brilliance, and Bill Kreutzmann’s shimmering cymbals. The momentum never wanes; after the marathon they drop into a bright “Sugar Magnolia” and finish with an uplifting “Casey Jones”. Across three soundboard and audience recordings reviewers consistently praise the tight interplay, the fluid solos (especially Bob Weir’s tasty lines on “Eyes”) and the pristine mix of the Miller SBD capture, making this set a must‑listen snapshot of early ’73 Dead at their improvisational peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings carry a positive sentiment; multiple reviewers call the performance "legendary", note Jerry’s flawless tone, Phil’s brilliant bass, and highlight the He’s Gone → Truckin’ → The Other One → Eyes → China Doll sequence as exceptional. No negative comments about playing appear, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard mix with no dropouts; captures the full second set in excellent fidelity, preserving every nuance of the marathon jam.

key_highlights: He’s Gone → Truckin’ → The Other One → Eyes Of The World → China Doll medley
Jerry’s flawless tone and emotive vocal on China Doll
Phil’s “kicking ass” bass brilliance throughout the set
Pristine Miller SBD recording quality makes this the definitive source

band_performance.Jerry: Flawless tone and emotive guitar work; his playing is described as perfect, especially on China Doll where his vocal delivery shines.
band_performance.Phil: Kicking ass on bass with lead‑bass brilliance; highlighted for interesting licks, solo zones, and a "killer Phil zone" throughout the extended jams.
band_performance.Bob: Tasty, fluid guitar work on Eyes Of The World, earning specific praise for his contribution to that segment.
band_performance.Billy: Cymbals shimmer and crash; Bill’s drumming is noted as slightly different but effective, driving the marathon set forward.
